questions.q: Which part of speech names a person, place, thing, or idea?
A word that typically modifies a noun, such as 'blue' or 'large', is called what?
What is the past tense of the irregular verb 'run'?
What is the plural of the word 'mouse' (the rodent)?
Which of these is a pronoun?
The punctuation mark '?' is called a what?
What is the plural form of 'child'?
What is the correct preposition to fill in the blank: 'I am proud _______ my achievements'?
Which article correctly completes this sentence: 'I go to the gym _______ week'?
What is the -ing form (gerund) of the verb 'to run'?
Which pronoun should replace the blank: 'This book is _______. It belongs to me.'?
How do you correctly form a question with the verb 'to go' in present tense with 'they'?
What is the correct preposition in this sentence: 'She comes _______ France'?
Which verb form correctly completes: 'Right now, he _______ (to read) a book'?
What is the correct way to describe a length measurement: 'The rope is _______'?
Which preposition correctly completes: 'I am _______ your team'?
What is the correct form to complete: 'They _______ (to have) dinner when we arrived'?
Which possessive pronoun correctly fills the blank: 'These pencils are _______'?
What is the correct comparative form of the adjective 'good'?
How do you correctly form a negative with 'to be' in present tense with 'she'?
Which reflexive pronoun correctly completes: 'I hurt _______'?
What is the correct preposition in this sentence: 'He is standing _______ one leg'?
Which verb form is correct: 'She _______ (to work) in that office for five years'?
What is the correct way to express quantity: '_______ of the students passed the exam'?
Which subject pronoun correctly replaces 'Tom and Sarah' in: '_______ are friends'?
What is the correct form of the verb in: 'Listen! Someone _______ (to knock) at the door'?
Which article should be used before a singular countable noun that starts with a consonant sound?
What is the correct form of the verb 'to be' in the sentence 'She ___ a teacher'?
In the sentence 'They ___ playing chess right now,' which verb form is correct?
Which word is an adjective in the sentence 'The quick brown fox jumps over the fence'?
Which sentence uses the correct possessive pronoun?
In the sentence 'I ___ to the store yesterday,' which past tense verb is correct?
Which word should complete the sentence 'There ___ ten students in the class'?
In the sentence 'She walks ___ to work every day,' which adverb fits best?
Which sentence uses the correct subject-verb agreement?
choices: The dog run in the park
The dogs runs in the park
The dog runs in the park
The dogs running in the park

questions.q: What is the superlative form of the adjective 'bad'?
In the sentence 'We ___ going to the beach tomorrow,' which auxiliary verb is correct?
Which word is a preposition in the sentence 'The cat sat under the table'?
What is the correct past participle of the verb 'eat'?
In the sentence 'He does ___ like vegetables,' which word completes it correctly?
Which article should be used before 'umbrella' in the sentence '___ umbrella is expensive'?
In the sentence 'Neither the teacher nor the students ___ ready,' which verb form is correct?
Which of these sentences uses the correct form of the present continuous tense?
choices: She be playing the guitar right now.
She playing the guitar right now.
She is playing the guitar right now.
She are playing the guitar right now.

questions.q: What is the correct superlative form of the adjective 'beautiful'?
Which sentence correctly uses a reflexive pronoun?
choices: Tom fixed the car by themself.
Tom fixed the car by his.
Tom fixed the car by himself.
Tom fixed the car by him.

questions.q: In the sentence 'I gave the book to her,' what part of speech is 'her'?
Which word is an example of an uncountable noun?
What is the correct comparative form of 'tall'?
Which sentence correctly uses the simple past tense?
choices: She going to the store yesterday.
She goes to the store yesterday.
She go to the store yesterday.
She went to the store yesterday.

questions.q: In a question tag, what is the correct ending for 'They play tennis'?
Which sentence correctly uses the future tense with 'will'?
choices: I will going to Paris next month.
I will went to Paris next month.
I will goes to Paris next month.
I will go to Paris next month.

questions.q: What is the correct form to complete: 'The dog is playing _______ the cat'?
Which sentence uses the correct possessive form?
In the sentence 'Does she like pizza?', what is the grammatical function of 'does'?
Which sentence correctly completes: 'Mary enjoys _______ books'?
What is the correct article to use in: '_____ apple a day keeps the doctor away'?
In the sentence 'I can do it myself,' what type of pronoun is 'myself'?
Which sentence correctly uses the present simple tense?
choices: He are working in an office.
He is work in an office.

questions.q: Which word is a countable noun?
In the sentence 'This is my brother's house,' what does the apostrophe indicate?
Which sentence correctly uses 'between'?
choices: The secret is between you, him, and I.
The secret is between you or him.
The secret is between you, him, and me.
The secret is among you and him.

questions.q: Which word is a noun in the sentence: 'The cat quickly jumped over the fence'?
What is the correct past tense form of the verb 'to eat'?
What is the -ing form of the verb 'to dance'?
Which word is an adjective in the phrase: 'a beautiful sunset'?
Which sentence correctly uses a comma?
choices: I like, apples oranges and bananas
I like apples oranges, and bananas
I like apples, oranges, and bananas
I like apples oranges and bananas

questions.q: What part of speech is the word 'quickly' in the sentence: 'She ran quickly to the door'?
Which sentence correctly uses an apostrophe?
choices: The dog bone's was buried in the yard
The dogs bone was buried in the yard
The dogs' bone was buried in the yard
The dog's bone was buried in the yard

questions.q: What is the correct form of the verb in: 'If I _______ time, I will help you'?
Which word in this sentence is a preposition: 'The book is on the table'?
What is the correct present tense form for: 'She _______ coffee every morning'?
Which word is a verb in the sentence: 'The students study hard for exams'?
What is the past participle of the verb 'to go'?
Which sentence correctly matches the subject with its verb?
Which of these sentences uses the correct present simple form with a third-person singular subject?
choices: She going to the market every Saturday.
She goes to the market every Saturday.
She go to the market every Saturday.
She gone to the market every Saturday.

questions.q: What is the correct form of the verb 'to be' in this sentence: 'They ____ students at the university.'?
Which word correctly completes this sentence: 'I have ____ appointment tomorrow afternoon.'?
What is the -ing form of the verb 'to sit'?
Which sentence correctly uses a possessive adjective?
choices: That is hers book, not his book.
That is her's book, not his book.
That is her book, not his book.
That is she book, not he book.

questions.q: Complete this sentence with the correct preposition: 'The cat is sitting ____ the sofa.'
What is the correct present continuous form for this sentence: 'Right now, she ____ a report.'?
Which word correctly completes this question tag: 'You like pizza, ____?'
Choose the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement:
choices: The data are showing good results.
The group of students are studying together.
The team are winning the match.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses an object pronoun?
choices: I gave the letter to she.
I gave the letter to her.
I gave the letter to hers.
I gave the letter to herself.

questions.q: Complete this sentence with the correct form: 'We ____ never been to Japan before.'
Which sentence uses the correct reflexive pronoun?
An ellipsis, used to indicate an omission or pause, consists of how many dots?
In traditional grammar, how many parts of speech are there in English?
The subjunctive mood in English is used to express what?
What name is given to a word or phrase that modifies the wrong element in a sentence (e.g. 'Running to the bus, my hat flew off')?
In traditional grammar, which of the following best describes a gerund?
choices: A noun formed from a verb by adding -ing
A participle that modifies the subject of a sentence
An auxiliary verb used in continuous tenses
A verb form that functions as an adjective

questions.q: Which sentence demonstrates correct use of the subjunctive mood in English?
choices: If I am you, I would reconsider that decision
If I were you, I would reconsider that decision
If I will be you, I would reconsider that decision
If I was you, I would reconsider that decision

questions.q: What grammatical term describes the relationship between two clauses in this sentence: 'Although she studied hard, she failed the exam'?
choices: Coordinate clauses joined by a conjunction
Two independent clauses with no relationship
Subordinate clause dependent on a main clause

questions.q: In the phrase 'the running water,' which grammatical function does 'running' serve?
choices: Auxiliary verb in the continuous aspect
Present participle functioning as an adjective

questions.q: Which sentence contains a dangling modifier?
choices: Excited about the concert, she bought her tickets early
Having finished her homework, Sarah went to bed
Walking through the park, the dog enjoyed the fresh air
Having lost his keys, the door remained locked

questions.q: In the sentence 'Neither the teacher nor the students were prepared,' which rule of subject-verb agreement applies?
choices: The verb agrees with the subject furthest from it
The verb agrees with the nearest subject
The verb always uses singular form with 'neither'
Plural agreement is required when 'nor' joins subjects

questions.q: Which of the following demonstrates correct parallel structure?
choices: She likes running, swimming, and playing tennis
She likes running, to swim, and playing tennis
She likes to run, to swim, and playing tennis
She likes to run, swimming, and to play tennis

questions.q: What grammatical concept is illustrated in this sentence: 'The committee has made its decision'?
choices: Collective noun treated as singular unit

questions.q: In prescriptive grammar, which sentence is considered grammatically correct?
choices: Who you saw at the market?
Whom you saw at the market?
Whom did you see at the market?
Who did you see at the market?

questions.q: What is the grammatical function of the phrase 'with remarkable speed' in: 'She solved the puzzle with remarkable speed'?
choices: Noun phrase serving as direct object
Prepositional phrase functioning as an adjective

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses the future perfect tense?
choices: By next year, she will have completed her degree
By next year, she will complete her degree
By next year, she had completed her degree
By next year, she completes her degree

questions.q: In this sentence, 'The books that were damaged in the flood were replaced,' what is the antecedent of the relative pronoun 'that'?
What is the term for a preposition that follows a verb, such as 'put up with' or 'run into'?
Which sentence demonstrates an error in pronoun-antecedent agreement?
choices: Each student must bring their own materials
Everyone in the class completed their assignments
Neither player gave their best effort
Someone left their umbrella in the office

questions.q: What grammatical feature does the prefix 'un-' in the word 'unhappy' exemplify?
In the sentence 'Having considered all options, the board voted unanimously,' what is the grammatical status of the opening phrase?
choices: Absolute phrase with a dangling participle

questions.q: Which of these sentences correctly uses the past perfect tense in context?
choices: By the time we arrived, they are leaving the building
By the time we arrived, they had left the building
By the time we arrived, they left the building
By the time we arrived, they leave the building

questions.q: What grammatical principle is violated in: 'The manager, along with his assistants, are reviewing the proposal'?
In traditional grammar, what is a 'split infinitive' and when is it considered problematic?
choices: Two infinitives used in the same clause; always incorrect
An infinitive used at the end of a sentence; informally acceptable
A passive infinitive; only problematic in formal writing
An adverb placed between 'to' and the verb; prescriptively discouraged

questions.q: In the sentence 'The committee has made its decision,' what is the grammatical function of the word 'has'?
Which sentence demonstrates the correct use of the dangling modifier?
choices: The sunset, walking through the park, was beautiful.
Walking through the park, beautiful was the sunset.
While walking through the park, she admired the sunset.
Walking through the park, the sunset was beautiful.

questions.q: In prescriptive grammar, the phrase 'between you and I' is considered incorrect. What is the correct form?
Which of the following correctly uses a semicolon according to standard English punctuation rules?
choices: The meeting was postponed; however, we still attended.
The meeting was postponed; however we still attended.
The meeting was postponed however; we still attended.
The meeting was postponed, however; we still attended.

questions.q: In the sentence 'Neither the director nor the actors were available,' what is the grammatical error?
choices: The verb 'were' should be 'was' because the subject is singular.
There is no error; the sentence is grammatically correct.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses the restrictive relative clause without a comma?
choices: The book which I read last week was fascinating.
The book that I read, last week, was fascinating.
The book, which I read last week, was fascinating.
The book that, I read last week, was fascinating.

questions.q: What is the grammatical term for a word that has more than one part of speech depending on context, such as 'read' (present and past)?
In the phrase 'The data are consistent with our hypothesis,' why is 'are' correct rather than 'is'?
choices: Data can be treated as either singular or plural, but 'are' is preferred in formal scientific writing.
The verb must agree with the nearest noun, which is plural.
'Are' is incorrect; 'is' is the only acceptable form.
Data is always plural in modern English.

questions.q: In traditional grammar, what is the primary difference between 'who' and 'whom'?
choices: 'Who' is used for subjects and 'whom' is used for objects.
'Who' refers to people and 'whom' refers to things.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses the gerund as the object of a preposition?
choices: He is interested in to learn about linguistics.
He is interested in learning about linguistics.
He is interested in to have learned about linguistics.
He is interested in learned about linguistics.

questions.q: What is the grammatical name for the word 'very' in the phrase 'very quickly'?
In the sentence 'Having finished the report, the team celebrated their success,' what type of phrase is 'Having finished the report'?
Which of the following correctly demonstrates the use of the Oxford comma in a series?
choices: I bought apples, oranges and bananas.
I bought apples, oranges, and bananas.
I bought apples; oranges; and bananas.

questions.q: In formal English grammar, what is the error in 'Each of the students have completed their assignments'?
choices: The pronoun 'their' should be 'his or her' because 'each' is singular.
The verb 'have' should be 'has' to agree with the singular subject 'each'.
Both A and B are errors.
There is no error; the sentence follows modern English conventions.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses an ellipsis to show understood words?
choices: She likes coffee; he likes... tea.
She likes coffee; he likes tea that way.
She likes coffee; he likes tea...

questions.q: What grammatical term describes the relationship between 'John' and 'the teacher' in the sentence 'John, the teacher, will arrive soon'?
In which sentence is the subjunctive mood correctly used for a wish or contrary-to-fact condition?
choices: I wish I would be taller.

questions.q: Which of the following sentences correctly uses a conditional clause with proper verb tense sequencing?
choices: If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
If you will study hard, you will pass the exam.
If you studied hard, you will pass the exam.
If you had studied hard, you will pass the exam.

questions.q: In the sentence 'Neither the manager nor the employees were satisfied with the outcome,' what is the grammatical error?
choices: The verb 'were' is correct; there is no error.
The conjunction 'nor' should be replaced with 'or'.
The verb should be 'was' to agree with the nearest subject.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly demonstrates the use of a dangling modifier?
choices: Running through the park, the dogs chased the rabbits.
Running through the park quickly, the jogger felt energized.
Running through the park, many birds were observed.
Running through the park, Sarah saw many birds.

questions.q: What is the correct interpretation of the phrase 'only I saw the movie' versus 'I only saw the movie'?
choices: Both sentences mean the same thing.
The first emphasizes that no one else saw it; the second emphasizes that seeing was the only action taken.
The first is grammatically correct; the second is incorrect.
The second sentence contains a split infinitive error.

questions.q: In formal English, which of the following uses 'whom' correctly?
choices: The candidate to whom I referred lost the debate.
Whom do you think will win the election?
She asked whom was responsible for the error.
Whom is calling on the telephone?

questions.q: Which sentence contains a misplaced modifier that changes the intended meaning?
choices: The researcher discovered the fossil while excavating the site.
The researcher, while excavating, discovered the fossil.
While excavating the site, the fossil was discovered by the researcher.
The fossil, while excavating the site, was discovered.

questions.q: What grammatical term describes a noun phrase that renames another noun, as in 'My friend Sarah is an architect'?
In the sentence 'The data suggests that inflation is rising,' which of the following is true?
choices: 'Data' can be either singular or plural depending on context.
'Data' requires the auxiliary 'have' before the main verb.
'Data' is singular and requires a singular verb.
'Data' is plural and the verb should be 'suggest' in formal usage.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses a restrictive relative clause?
choices: The students, who submitted their assignments late, received extensions.
The students submitted assignments, who were late, and received extensions.
The students who submitted their assignments late received extensions.
The students who, submitted their assignments late, received extensions.

questions.q: What is the grammatical error in the sentence 'Having finished dinner, the movie began'?
choices: The conjunction 'and' is missing between the clauses.
The article 'the' is used incorrectly before 'movie'.
The participial phrase dangles; it does not clearly modify the subject.
The tense shifts from past to present.

questions.q: In traditional grammar, what is the function of the gerund in the sentence 'Swimming is excellent exercise'?
Which of the following sentences correctly uses parallel structure?
choices: The committee decided revising the budget, postponing the meeting, and to delay hiring.
The committee decided to revise the budget, postpone the meeting, and that hiring would be delayed.
The committee decided that it would revise, postpone, and hiring delays.
The committee decided to revise the budget, to postpone the meeting, and to delay hiring.

questions.q: In the context of verb tense sequencing, what is the correct past tense form in this sentence: 'By the time he arrived, she _______ the presentation'?
What is the grammatical distinction between 'who' and 'that' as relative pronouns?
choices: 'Who' is used for people; 'that' is used for things and animals.
'Who' introduces restrictive clauses; 'that' introduces non-restrictive clauses.
'That' can introduce both restrictive and non-restrictive clauses; 'who' introduces only restrictive clauses.
'Who' can reference both people and things; 'that' only refers to people in formal usage.

questions.q: In the sentence 'The board of directors have made their decision,' which grammatical principle is being applied?
choices: The noun 'board' is treated as a collective noun requiring plural agreement.
The auxiliary 'have' indicates a passive voice construction.
The prepositional phrase 'of directors' functions as an appositive.
The pronoun 'their' requires the verb to be plural.

questions.q: Which sentence contains an ambiguous antecedent?
choices: The students studied hard for their final examination.
The cat ate its dinner quickly.
The manager told the employee that she must complete the report.
The employees submitted their reports on time.

questions.q: What is the correct use of the infinitive after the verb 'persuade'?
choices: I persuaded him about leaving early.
I persuaded him to leave early.
I persuaded him for leaving early.

questions.q: In grammar, what is the term for a sentence that contains two independent clauses joined by a coordinating conjunction?
Which sentence demonstrates correct use of the conditional perfect tense?
choices: If you studied harder, you would have passed the exam.
If you had studied harder, you would pass the exam.
If you study harder, you would have passed the exam.
If you had studied harder, you would have passed the exam.

questions.q: Which of the following sentences demonstrates correct use of the subjunctive mood in English?
choices: She acts as if she is the manager of the company.
If I was you, I would reconsider that decision.
He demanded that we are ready by noon.
I suggest that he be present at the meeting.

questions.q: In the sentence 'The data shows consistent results,' what is the grammatical reason this construction is considered standard in modern English, despite 'data' being a Latin plural?
choices: English requires all Latin words to use Latin grammar rules.
Latin plurals are always treated as singular in English.
Data has been adopted as a mass noun in contemporary usage.
The verb 'shows' is conjugated incorrectly on purpose.

questions.q: What grammatical error is demonstrated in this sentence: 'The committee have made their decision'?
choices: Subject-verb agreement is violated in American English.
There is no error; both constructions are grammatically valid.
The verb tense is incorrect for collective nouns.
The possessive pronoun should be singular.

questions.q: In transforming active voice to passive voice, which element becomes the subject of the passive construction?
choices: The prepositional phrase of the active sentence
The indirect object of the active sentence
The direct object of the active sentence
The adverbial modifier of the active sentence

questions.q: Which sentence demonstrates correct parallelism in a compound structure?
choices: The report was thorough, well-researched, and it was presented clearly.
The manager praised the team for their efficiency, dedication, and creative thinking.
He plans to travel, seeing new places, and experiencing different cultures.
She enjoys reading novels, watching films, and to attend concerts.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses the past perfect tense to show the sequence of events?
choices: They were leaving when I called them.
I have been waiting for two hours.
She had completed her degree before she moved to Paris.
By the time he arrives, I finish all my work.

questions.q: In prescriptive grammar, what is the distinction between 'who' and 'whom' in the sentence 'The candidate _____ the interviewer selected was well-qualified'?
choices: Who must be used with relative clauses.
Either is acceptable in modern usage.
Who is correct because it is the subject of 'was'.
Whom is correct because it is the object of 'selected'.

questions.q: What is the grammatical function of the infinitive phrase in 'The goal is to increase productivity'?
In the phrase 'a number of students,' which verb form is grammatically correct?
choices: A number of students has completed the assignment.
A number of students have completed the assignment.
Both forms are equally correct in all contexts.
Neither form is acceptable in formal writing.

questions.q: What grammatical phenomenon is demonstrated in the sentence 'She is taller than I'?
choices: Ellipsis of the verb in the second clause

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses an absolute phrase?
choices: Running quickly, he arrived on time.
To succeed, one must study diligently.
The meeting being concluded early, we had time for lunch.

questions.q: Which of these sentences correctly uses a restrictive appositive?
choices: The poet Emily Dickinson, who lived in Massachusetts, rarely left her home.
My sister Sarah moved to Denver.
Our neighbor, an engineer, designed the new bridge.
My brother Tom, whom you met last year, works in finance.

questions.q: What is the correct antecedent for 'which' in the sentence 'The report discussed several initiatives, which the board approved'?
choices: The entire independent clause preceding the relative clause

questions.q: In formal grammar, which sentence demonstrates correct use of the split infinitive debate in modern prescriptive standards?
choices: Modern style guides accept split infinitives when clarity is improved.
To quickly run is better than to walk slowly.
Never to under any circumstances split an infinitive.
He decided to boldly go where none had gone before.

questions.q: Which sentence demonstrates proper agreement with the indefinite pronoun 'everyone'?
choices: Everyone are required to submit their assignments.
Everyone need to bring their own materials.
Everyone is required to submit their assignment.
Everyone have completed their work on time.

questions.q: In the sentence 'The data shows that employee retention has improved,' what is the grammatical number of the subject?
In formal grammar, which of these sentences correctly uses the subjunctive form in a conditional clause?
choices: Should you will need assistance, you can call our office.
Should you need assistance, you can call our office.
Should you are needing assistance, you can call our office.
Should you needed assistance, you can call our office.

questions.q: Which sentence demonstrates correct pronoun case with a gerund?
choices: The teacher appreciated his study so diligently.
The teacher appreciated he studying so diligently.
The teacher appreciated him studying so diligently.
The teacher appreciated his studying so diligently.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses an appositive phrase?
choices: My friend Sarah that is a doctor works at the hospital.
My friend Sarah, a doctor, works at the hospital.
My friend Sarah being a doctor works at the hospital.
My friend Sarah, who is a doctor, works at the hospital.

questions.q: What is the correct antecedent for the pronoun in: 'Although the committee reviewed the proposals, they were incomplete'?
In the sentence 'The team are divided on the issue,' which version reflects American English conventions?
choices: The team is divided on the issue.
The team are divided on the issue.
The team members is divided on the issue.
The teams are divided on the issue.

questions.q: What is the grammatical function of the phrase 'to understand the problem' in: 'To understand the problem requires patience'?
Which of these sentences contains a split infinitive?
choices: She decided carefully to read the instructions.
She carefully decided to read the instructions.
She decided to read the instructions carefully.
She decided to carefully read the instructions.

questions.q: In formal English, which construction is grammatically correct?
choices: The reason why he left is because he was unhappy.
The reason for which he left is because he was unhappy.
The reason for why he left is that he was unhappy.
The reason he left is that he was unhappy.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly demonstrates parallel structure?
choices: She enjoys to read, writing, and painting.
She enjoys reading, to write, and painting.
She enjoys reading, writing, and to paint.
She enjoys reading, writing, and painting.

questions.q: What is the correct form when using the correlative conjunction 'not only...but also'?
choices: He is not only talented but also intelligent.
He not only is talented but also is intelligent.
He is not only talented but intelligent also.
He is not only talented but also he is intelligent.

questions.q: In the sentence 'Everyone should bring their own lunch,' what is the traditional grammar issue?
choices: The pronoun 'their' should be 'his' to match the singular antecedent.
The verb 'should' is incorrect for the subject.
There is no grammatical issue in modern English.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses the past perfect tense in sequence?
choices: By the time she arrived, he had left.
By the time she arrives, he will leave.
By the time she had arrived, he had left.
By the time she arrives, he left.

questions.q: What grammatical category does the word 'very' belong to in: 'The solution was very obvious'?
In the phrase 'The minister's speech was well-received,' what part of speech is 'well-received'?
Which sentence correctly uses subjunctive mood to express a hypothetical situation?
choices: If he will study harder, he would pass the exam.
If he had studied harder, he will pass the exam.
If he studies harder, he will pass the exam.
If he studied harder, he would pass the exam.

questions.q: Which sentence correctly demonstrates the use of the subjunctive mood in English?
choices: If he were the manager, he would implement new policies.
If he was the manager, he would implement new policies.
If he is the manager, he will implement new policies.
If he would be the manager, he implements new policies.

questions.q: What is the grammatical term for a word that modifies a noun but does not precede it directly, as in 'The solution, elegant and comprehensive, satisfied everyone'?
In the phrase 'The data suggests that the hypothesis is flawed,' why is the singular verb 'suggests' grammatically correct despite 'data' being a plural form?
choices: Because Latin plurals require singular verbs in English
Because 'data' has become accepted as a mass noun in modern English usage
Because verbs must always agree with the object, not the subject
Because the subject is actually 'hypothesis,' not 'data'

questions.q: What is a word that modifies a verb, adjective, or another adverb called?
The so-called 'Oxford comma' is used before the word 'and' in which context?
choices: Before the final item in a list
After a greeting in a letter

questions.q: A word that sounds like the thing it describes, such as 'buzz' or 'sizzle', is an example of what?
Of the three words 'they're, their, there', which one is the possessive pronoun?
A figure of speech that compares two unlike things using 'like' or 'as' is called what?
What is the term for the repetition of initial consonant sounds across words, as in 'Peter Piper picked'?
What do we call words like 'and', 'but', and 'or' that join words or clauses together?
An 'oxymoron' is a figure of speech combining two words that are what?
What is the superlative form of the adjective 'good'?
Which part of speech expresses a strong feeling, often followed by an exclamation point (e.g. 'Ouch!', 'Wow!')?
Which of the following is a countable noun?
Select the sentence with correct subject-verb agreement.
choices: Neither of the options are correct.
The team are playing well together.

questions.q: Which possessive pronoun correctly completes this sentence: 'This notebook is _____, not yours.'?
What is the correct form of the present continuous tense for 'I (work) in the garden right now'?
choices: I will work in the garden
I am working in the garden
I have worked in the garden

questions.q: Which sentence correctly uses the preposition 'on'?
choices: The picture is on the wall.
The book is on the table.
All of the above are correct.

questions.q: Identify the correct objective pronoun in: 'Can you give _____ the documents?'
Which form completes this sentence correctly: 'She is considering _____ (to buy) a new house.'?
What is the plural possessive form of 'child'?
Which sentence demonstrates correct use of the relative pronoun 'that'?
choices: The book that I reading is interesting.
The book what I am reading is interesting.
The book where I am reading is interesting.
The book that I am reading is interesting.

questions.q: Select the sentence with proper article usage.
Identify the correct form: 'If I _____ (to know) the answer, I would tell you.'
Which sentence uses the past continuous tense correctly?
